engineGuide.h2: BMW M5 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW M5 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: The original M5 powerplant, derived from the M1 supercar. A twin-cam straight-six that set the template for high-performance saloons.
commonFailures: Timing chain wear (100,000+ miles)
Head gasket failure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M88/3 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Evolution of the M88 with a longer stroke crankshaft and variable-length intake manifold. Built for the first-generation E34 M5.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear
Valve clearance adjustment drift
entries.cta: Get quotes for S38B36 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: The ultimate evolution of BMW's straight-six M engine. Displacement increased to 3.8 litres with dual-row timing chains for added durability.
commonFailures: VANOS solenoid failure
Timing chain guide wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for S38B38 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.9L Petrol
entries.history: The first V8 M5 engine, producing 400hp from a naturally aspirated 4.9-litre. Known for its "growling" induction noise and enthusiastic following among collectors.
commonFailures: VANOS solenoid and seal failure - causes rough idle, growling noise, power loss
Timing chain stretch and guide wear (diesel-like rattle on start-up)
Camshaft sensor failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for S62B50 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: The only production V10 ever fitted to a saloon car. A 507hp naturally aspirated masterpiece with individual throttle bodies and a 8,250rpm redline - now a modern classic sought after by collectors.
commonFailures: Rod bearing failure - essential replacement before 70,000 miles, catastrophic if ignored
Throttle actuator failure - symptoms include limp mode, drivetrain malfunction (£1,000+ repair)
VANOS oil pump failure - triggers oil pressure warning lights
SMG pump failure - gearbox malfunction, won't engage gears
entries.cta: Get quotes for S85B50 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: BMW's first turbocharged M5 engine, producing 560hp from a 4.4-litre V8 with twin turbos. Replaced the V10 and fundamentally changed the M5's character toward effortless torque.
commonFailures: Spun rod bearings - bottom end knocking noise
Timing chain guide failure - rattle on cold start
High-pressure fuel pump failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for S63B44 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: Updated "Technical Update" version of the S63. Revised turbos, cooling system, and ECU mapping - fitted to the F10 Competition Package cars producing 575hp.
compatibleVariants: M5 (F10 Competition), M5 (F10 LCI), M5 (F10 30 Jahre)
commonFailures: Timing chain guide failure - engine rattle
Coolant leak from valley pan - overheating
Turbocharger wastegate rattle
entries.cta: Get quotes for S63B44T2 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol
entries.history: The most powerful S63 variant to date, fitted to the F90 M5. Produces 600hp in standard form and up to 635hp in the limited-run CS. Features cross-bank exhaust manifolds and upgraded turbos.
compatibleVariants: M5 (F90), M5 (F90 Competition), M5 (F90 LCI), M5 (F90 CS)
commonFailures: Oil pump drive failure - oil pressure light
Coolant leak (valley pan)
Fuel injector failure - misfire
entries.cta: Get quotes for S63B44T4 BMW M5 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4L Petrol Hybrid
entries.history: The most powerful production M5 ever, combining the S68 V8 with a plug-in hybrid system. Total system output of 727hp marks the beginning of the electrified M era.
commonFailures: High-voltage battery degradation (long-term prediction)
Electric motor cooling system leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for S68B44 PHEV BMW M5 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW M5 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW M5’s reputation for high‑performance engineering comes with well‑documented engine vulnerabilities, particularly in the V10 S85 and twin‑turbo V8 S63 families. These two engine families dominate replacement demand, with rod bearing failures in the E60 generation and timing chain / oil pump issues in the F10 and F90 cars accounting for the majority of enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below we break down the most common failures, what repair (or replacement) actually costs through dealers versus specialists, and when it makes financial sense to act.

problems.group: BMW M5 S85 V10 Rod Bearing Failure
problems.h4: BMW M5 S85 V10 Rod Bearing Failure - Why E60 M5 Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: BMW M5 E60, M5 E61 Touring (2005‑2010, S85B50)
problems.rootCause: Insufficient rod bearing clearance from the factory, leading to oil film breakdown under hard cornering or extended high‑RPM running. Bearings eventually spin, seize to the crankshaft, and cause bottom‑end destruction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rod bearings with aftermarket uprated units (e.g., ACL or WPC‑treated), new bolts, oil pump, sump gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 60,000‑80,000 miles of safe life if done before any knocking noise or low oil pressure warning

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S85B50 with uprated rod bearings, new timing chain, VANOS overhaul, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: New engine life with upgraded components; 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: On an E60 M5 worth £15,000‑£25,000, preventative bearing replacement is the single best investment you can make. If you hear bottom‑end knock or see low oil pressure warnings, the engine is already terminal - replacement is the only safe option. Do not attempt a “half rebuild” on a spun bearing S85.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S85B50 engine prices for your BMW M5 E60

problems.group: BMW M5 S63 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW M5 S63 Timing Chain Failure - F10 V8 Rattle That Leads to Engine Seizure
problems.affectedModels: BMW M5 F10 (2011‑2013, S63B44); M5 F10 Competition, F10 LCI, F10 30 Jahre (2012‑2016, S63B44T2)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guide rails become brittle with heat cycling, fracture, and allow chain slack. The chain can jump timing, skip teeth, or snap entirely - causing valve‑to‑piston contact and cylinder head destruction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, guides, tensioners, camshaft sprockets, and VANOS units (while accessible)
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; adds 60,000‑80,000 miles of reliable service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S63B44 or S63B44T2 with upgraded timing components, new turbochargers, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: New engine life with revised guides; 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: Listen for a diesel‑like rattle on cold start or a metallic chatter on acceleration - these are warning signs. Replace chains and guides immediately. If the engine has jumped timing or snapped a chain, replacement is cheaper than attempting a cylinder head rebuild on an S63.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S63B44 timing chain repair or replacement for your F10 M5

problems.group: BMW M5 S85 Throttle Actuator Failure
problems.h4: BMW M5 S85 Throttle Actuator Failure - E60 V10 Limp Mode That Mimics Engine Death
problems.affectedModels: BMW M5 E60, M5 E61 Touring (2005‑2010, S85B50)
problems.rootCause: DC electric motors inside the throttle actuators wear out their internal gearbox or electronics fail, causing the ECU to cut power to multiple cylinders. The car enters limp mode with “Drivetrain Malfunction” warning - often mistaken for total engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both throttle actuator units (banks 1 and 2) with rebuilt or OEM‑spec units; ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full power; rebuilt actuators with upgraded gears last 60,000+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S85B50 - only if ignored actuators allowed other failures (rod bearings, timing)
repairOptions.longevity: Only required if the car was driven repeatedly in limp mode, causing secondary damage

problems.recommendation: This is not an engine replacement problem - it’s a bolt‑on component failure. Replace both actuators proactively if you own an E60 M5, even before failure. If you’re already in limp mode, a full rebuilt engine is almost never needed unless you ignored it for thousands of miles.
problems.cta: Compare S85 throttle actuator repair vs engine replacement costs for your E60 M5

problems.group: BMW M5 S63 Oil Pump Drive Failure
problems.h4: BMW M5 S63 Oil Pump Drive Failure - F90 V8 Oil Pressure Loss Leads to Bottom‑End Knock
problems.affectedModels: BMW M5 F90, F90 Competition, F90 LCI, F90 CS (2018‑2023, S63B44T4)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump drive shaft or chain tensioner fails, causing sudden oil pressure loss. The first warning is often the red oil pressure light - by then, bottom‑end bearings are already damaged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump assembly, drive chain, tensioner, and inspect bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if oil pressure light just appeared and engine hasn’t knocked; 50/50 success rate

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S63B44T4 with uprated oil pump drive, new bearings, turbocharger inspection

problems.recommendation: If you see the red oil pressure warning light on an F90 M5, do not drive another metre. Tow the car. If you stop immediately, an oil pump and bearing replacement may save the engine. If you hear knocking, go straight to full engine replacement - attempting to rebuild a damaged bottom end on an S63 is false economy.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S63B44T4 engine prices for your F90 M5

problems.h4: BMW M5 S62 VANOS Failure - E39 V8 Rough Idle, Growling Noise & Power Loss
problems.rootCause: VANOS solenoid seals harden and leak oil pressure, causing the camshaft timing to drift. The engine develops a distinctive “growling” sound at idle, hesitation on acceleration, and eventually fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VANOS solenoid seals (both banks), camshaft position sensors, and timing chain tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full power and idle quality; lasts 50,000‑70,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S62B50 with rebuilt VANOS units, timing chain, guides, rod bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Only if VANOS failure caused skipped timing and valve damage

problems.recommendation: This is a maintenance item, not a replacement trigger on a healthy E39 M5. Address VANOS seals early. If the engine still runs smoothly, the minor fix is excellent value. Only consider full replacement if the timing has jumped or you have bottom‑end knock from neglected rod bearings.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S62 VANOS repair or rebuilt E39 M5 engine prices

problems.group: BMW M5 S85 High Oil Consumption
problems.h4: BMW M5 S85 High Oil Consumption - V10 Burning a Litre Every 1,000 Miles
problems.affectedModels: BMW M5 E60, M5 E61 Touring (2005‑2010, S85B50)
problems.rootCause: Worn valve stem seals and carbon‑blocked piston rings, exacerbated by the S85’s 8,250rpm redline and 10W‑60 oil viscosity. Oil passes into combustion chambers, fouls spark plugs, and accelerates catalytic converter failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 40 valve stem seals (20 cylinders) without full engine removal; new head gaskets, timing tools
repairOptions.longevity: Reduces oil consumption by 80‑90%; adds 40,000‑60,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt S85B50 with new rings, valve seals, rod bearings, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 100% oil consumption fix; 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: On a high‑mileage E60 M5 burning significant oil, valve stem seals offer a cost‑effective middle ground. But if the car also has rod bearing wear or timing chain rattle, invest in a full rebuilt engine. Partial repairs on an S85 with multiple issues rarely add up to long‑term value.
problems.cta: Compare S85 oil consumption fix vs full rebuilt engine prices for your E60 M5

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW M5
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you own an E60 V10 with failing rod bearings, an F90 V8 with oil pump failure, or an E39 with VANOS issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK BMW M5 specialists. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded components that address the original failure point - not a like‑for‑like replacement waiting to fail again. With a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery & fitting options, you can get back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW M5 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW M5 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Bmw M5 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Dismantled, inspected, worn components replaced to factory tolerance. Not simply cleaned - genuinely reconditioned.

types.frontDisclaimer: Ideal for S63 and S85 V8/V10 engines with known bearing or timing chain failure.
types.backDescription: Factory-spec reconditioning of BMW M5 engines focuses on the S63 twin-turbo V8 and S85 V10 families.
backBullets: Every engine is stripped, ultrasonically cleaned and measured against BMW tolerances
New piston rings, bearings, seals, timing chains and oil pumps fitted as standard
12-month unlimited mileage warranty included with all reconditioned units

types.title: Bmw M5 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Full strip-down, machining, replacement of all wear items. Higher standard than reconditioned.
types.frontDescription: Full strip-down, machining, replacement of all wear items. Higher standard than reconditioned.
types.frontDisclaimer: Recommended for high-performance S63B44T4 and S85B50 where original failures must be eliminated.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt M5 engines go beyond reconditioning with new critical components and upgraded parts.
backBullets: Crankshaft reground, cylinders rebored, cylinder head pressure-tested and skimmed
Uprated rod bearings fitted (S85) or upgraded timing chain guides (S63)
Every rebuilt unit bench-tested before delivery and comes with 12-month warranty

types.title: Bmw M5 Used Engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Higher risk option - recommended only for budget-limited projects or temporary fixes.

No internal inspection or component replacement performed before sale

engineSizes.h2: BMW M5 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW M5 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes specific to each unit.

items.title: BMW M5 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original M5 powerplant, the 3.5-litre straight-six M88/3, powered the E28 M5 from 1985 to 1988. A twin-cam, four-valve-per-cylinder engine derived from the M1 supercar, it established the high-revving, naturally aspirated character that defined early M5 generations. This displacement remains highly sought after by collectors and classic BMW specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain wear at higher mileages (100,000+ miles)
head gasket failure on overheated units
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The S38B36 displaced 3.6 litres and powered the early E34 M5 from 1988 to 1992. Building on the M88 architecture, it introduced a longer stroke crankshaft and a variable-length intake manifold for improved mid-range torque. This engine marked the transition toward more usable daily performance without sacrificing the straight-six's willingness to rev.
commonFailurePoints: valve clearance adjustment drift on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 3.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The final evolution of BMW's M straight-six, the 3.8-litre S38B38 appeared in the later E34 M5 from 1992 to 1995. Displacement increased through bore and stroke changes, while dual-row timing chains improved durability over earlier single-row designs. This engine remains a favourite among E34 enthusiasts, particularly in the rare M5 Touring estate.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain guide wear at 80,000-100,000 miles
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 3.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 defines modern M5 performance, appearing across the F10, F90 and G90 generations in multiple S63 and S68 variants. From 560hp in the early F10 to 600hp in the standard F90 and 727hp (hybrid-assisted) in the G90, this displacement covers the majority of M5s sold in the UK since 2011. Cooling system upgrades and cross-bank exhaust manifolds distinguish later versions.
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 4.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 4.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.9-litre S62B50 V8 powered the E39 M5 from 1998 to 2003, producing 400hp from a naturally aspirated 4.9-litre configuration. The first V8 M5 engine, it featured individual throttle butterflies, double-VANOS variable valve timing and an 8-cylinder growl that remains iconic among enthusiasts. This generation sits in a classic sweet spot - modern enough for daily use but analogue enough to feel special.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain guide wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 4.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary 5.0-litre V10 S85B50 remains unique in M5 history - the only production V10 ever fitted to a saloon car. Producing 507hp at 7,750rpm, it powered the E60 M5 and E61 M5 Touring from 2005 to 2010. Its Formula 1-inspired architecture, individual throttle bodies and 8,250rpm redline create an unparalleled naturally aspirated experience - but its well-documented bearing failures make replacement a common reality for owners.
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 5.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW M5 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW M5 4.4L Plug-In Hybrid Engines
items.description: The G90 M5 introduced a plug-in hybrid powertrain combining the S68 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 with an electric motor integrated into the eight-speed automatic gearbox. Total system output reaches 727hp with an electric-only range of approximately 40 miles, marking the first electrified M5. The hybrid system adds complexity and weight but delivers staggering performance while allowing zero-emission urban driving. Not sure which engine size your BMW M5 needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK BMW M5 specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW M5 4.4L plug-in hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: BMW M5 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your BMW M5 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the classic E28 M5 to the latest G90 plug-in hybrid.

items.title: BMW M5 Petrol Engines
items.description: Every BMW M5 ever sold in the UK has been petrol-powered, spanning six generations of straight-six, V8 and V10 engines - from the original M88/3 to the latest S68 plug-in hybrid.
items.descriptor: Every BMW M5 ever sold in the UK has been petrol-powered, spanning six generations of straight-six, V8 and V10 engines - from the original M88/3 to the latest S68 plug-in hybrid.
items.familiesLabel: Common BMW M5 Petrol Engine Families
families: S85B50 - 5.0L V10 (E60/E61), catastrophic rod bearing failures drive replacement demand
S63B44 - 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (F10), timing chain guide failure at 70k-100k miles
S63B44T2 - 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (F10 Competition/LCI), upgraded cooling and turbos
S63B44T4 - 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (F90/F90 Competition/CS), oil pump drive failure
S62B50 - 4.9L V8 (E39), VANOS seal failure and growling idle
S38B36 - 3.6L straight-six (E34 3.6), timing chain tensioner wear
M88/3 - 3.5L straight-six (E28), timing chain wear at high mileage
foundIn: F10 M5 Competition / LCI / 30 Jahre (2012-2016) - S63B44T2
G90 M5 / G90 Touring / G99 Touring (2024+) - S68B44 PHEV
knownFor: S85 V10 rod bearing failure - the most searched M5 engine replacement query in the UK
S63 timing chain guide failure across F10 and early F90 generations
S62 VANOS growl - a signature E39 M5 issue that drives preventative replacement
Higher specialist rebuild costs than volume brands - expect £6,000-£22,000 for rebuilt units
No diesel M5 ever produced - petrol remains the only factory fuel type
items.cta: Compare BMW M5 Petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW M5 Plug-In Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The G90 M5 introduced BMW's first electrified M5 powertrain - a plug-in hybrid combining the S68 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 with an electric motor integrated into the gearbox.
items.descriptor: The G90 M5 introduced BMW's first electrified M5 powertrain - a plug-in hybrid combining the S68 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 with an electric motor integrated into the gearbox.
families: S68B44 PHEV - 4.4L twin-turbo V8 with electric motor assist (G90/G99)
Electric motor mounted inside the 8-speed automatic gearbox provides instant torque fill
18.6kWh battery enables approximately 40 miles of electric-only driving
Petrol engine and electric motor combine for 727hp total system output
importantNotes: Hybrid system adds significant complexity compared to non-hybrid M5 engines
Replacement requires specialist hybrid-trained technicians and battery safety protocols
Availability of rebuilt PHEV units is currently limited - most replacements are dealer-supplied
Battery health inspection is mandatory during any engine replacement on PHEV models

engineYears.h2: BMW M5 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers every major engine change across the BMW M5's production timeline from 1985 to the present, helping owners identify their exact engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: E28 M5: The Original Super Saloon
years.description: The first-ever M5 launched at the Amsterdam Motor Show in February 1985, using an M1-derived straight-six that made it the world's fastest production saloon.
keyChanges: First M5 ever produced - hand-built at BMW Motorsport's Garching plant
M88/3 engine derived from the M1 supercar
Only 2,241 units built worldwide, making it among the rarest M cars
knownFor: The car that defined the sports saloon segment
286hp from a naturally aspirated straight-six - class-leading power in its era
Hand-built rarity - only 2,241 produced
Timing chain wear on high-mileage examples (100k+ miles)
years.cta: Get quotes for 1985-1988 BMW M5 E28 replacement engines

years.description: The third-generation 5 Series spawned this second M5, still using a straight-six but now with 315hp and improved mid-range torque.
keyChanges: New E34 platform with improved suspension and technology
S38B36 engine with longer stroke crankshaft and variable-length intake manifold
Still hand-assembled at M GmbH in Garching
knownFor: 315hp naturally aspirated straight-six
6.3-second 0-60mph time, 155mph limited top speed
Timing chain tensioner wear a known high-mileage issue
Valve clearance adjustment required periodically
years.cta: Get quotes for 1988-1992 BMW M5 E34 3.6 replacement engines

years.description: The 1992 update brought a larger 3.8-litre engine, 340hp, and - for the first time - an M5 Touring estate that remains a collector's unicorn.
keyChanges: Engine enlarged from 3.6 to 3.8 litres (S38B38)
Power increased to 340hp with 6-speed manual gearbox introduced
First M5 Touring estate launched - only 891 produced
Electronic Damper Control (EDC) introduced
mainEngines: S38B38 - 3.8L Petrol (E34 M5 3.8, E34 M5 Touring)
knownFor: 340hp, 0-60mph in 5.9 seconds, 174mph de-restricted top speed
M5 Touring is one of the rarest M cars - highly sought after by collectors
VANOS solenoid failure on bank 1 is a known weak point
Dual-row timing chains improved durability over 3.6-litre
years.cta: Get quotes for 1992-1995 BMW M5 E34 3.8 replacement engines

years.description: Widely regarded as one of the best M5s ever built, the E39 introduced a 400hp naturally aspirated V8 and remains an enthusiast favourite for its build quality and driving dynamics.
keyChanges: First M5 to use a V8 engine - the 4.9-litre S62
Moved from hand-built to production-line assembly at Dingolfing plant
Individual throttle bodies and double-VANOS variable valve timing
6-speed manual gearbox only - no automatic option
knownFor: 400hp, 0-60mph in 5.3 seconds, 155mph limited / 187mph de-restricted
VANOS seal failure causes growling idle and power loss - very common
Camshaft position sensor failure triggers check engine light
One of the most reliable M5 engines when properly maintained
20,482 units produced - far more than earlier generations
years.cta: Compare 1998-2003 BMW M5 E39 replacement engine prices
years.ticker: BMW M5 E39 engine replacement • S62 VANOS repair cost • E39 M5 engine rebuild price

years.description: The only production V10 ever fitted to a saloon car, derived from BMW's Williams F1 program. 507hp at 8,250rpm - but notorious rod bearing failures dominate replacement demand today.
keyChanges: All-new S85 V10 engine - 5.0 litres, 507hp, 8,250rpm redline
SMG III 7-speed sequential manual gearbox (6-speed manual in North America only)
E61 M5 Touring estate returned to the range (2007-2010)
iDrive infotainment system introduced
mainEngines: S85B50 - 5.0L Petrol (E60 M5, E61 M5 Touring)
knownFor: Rod bearing failure is critical - essential replacement before 70,000 miles
Throttle actuator failure causes limp mode - £1,000+ repair
VANOS oil pump failure leads to oil pressure warnings
SMG pump failure - gearbox won't engage gears
The highest "distress search" volume of any M5 engine - owners fear catastrophic failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2010 BMW M5 E60/E61 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW M5 E60 rod bearing failure • S85 V10 engine replacement cost • E61 M5 Touring engine • BMW M5 V10 throttle actuator repair
enquiries: BMW M5 E60 rod bearing failure
BMW M5 V10 throttle actuator repair

years.description: The fifth-generation M5 abandoned natural aspiration for twin-turbo V8 power - 560hp from a 4.4-litre S63, marking a fundamental shift in M5 character toward effortless torque.
keyChanges: First turbocharged M5 - S63B44 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8
560hp and 680Nm - massive torque from just 1,500rpm
Last rear-wheel-drive M5 - all subsequent generations gained AWD
M-DCT 7-speed dual-clutch gearbox replaced SMG
knownFor: 560hp, 0-60mph in 4.3 seconds, 155mph limited
Timing chain guide failure causes engine rattle - common around 70k-100k miles
Spun rod bearings reported on early S63 units
High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure known issue
Heavier than E60 but more usable daily performance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2013 BMW M5 F10 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW M5 F10 engine replacement • S63 timing chain failure • F10 M5 engine rattle • S63B44 rebuilt price

years.description: The F10 generation matured with Competition Package (575hp), 30 Jahre special edition (600hp), and LCI facelift - all using the updated S63B44T2 engine.
keyChanges: Competition Package added 15hp (575hp total) with revised suspension
30th Anniversary "30 Jahre" edition produced 600hp - only 300 units
LCI facelift (2013) brought cosmetic and infotainment updates
Final rear-wheel-drive M5 before F90's AWD switch
mainEngines: S63B44T2 - 4.4L Petrol (F10 M5 Competition, F10 M5 LCI, F10 M5 30 Jahre)
knownFor: Competition: 575hp, 0-60mph in 4.2 seconds
30 Jahre: 600hp, 0-60mph in 3.9 seconds - fastest rear-drive M5
Timing chain guide failure still present on TU engines
Turbocharger wastegate rattle common on higher-mileage units
Coolant leaks from valley pan reported
years.cta: Compare 2012-2016 BMW M5 F10 Competition replacement engine prices

years.description: The sixth-generation M5 introduced M xDrive all-wheel drive, finally taming the 600hp S63B44T4 V8 while allowing pure rear-drive mode for purists.
keyChanges: First AWD M5 with switchable M xDrive (can be pure RWD)
S63B44T4 engine - 600hp, 750Nm, 0-60mph in 3.4 seconds
New 8-speed M Steptronic gearbox replaces DCT
Revised chassis and suspension for AWD architecture
knownFor: 600hp, 0-60mph in 3.4 seconds, 155mph limited / 190mph with M Driver's Package
Oil pump drive failure - oil pressure light is a critical warning
Coolant leak from valley pan - common S63 issue
Fuel injector failure causes misfire
Much more usable in British weather thanks to AWD
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 BMW M5 F90 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW M5 F90 engine replacement • F90 M5 oil pump failure • S63B44T4 rebuild cost

years.description: The F90 reached its peak with Competition (625hp) and limited-edition CS (635hp) variants - the CS remains the most powerful production M5 before electrification.
keyChanges: Competition Package (2018+): 625hp, 0-60mph in 3.3 seconds, 190mph top speed
M5 CS (2021-2023): 635hp, 0-60mph in 3.0 seconds, carbon ceramic brakes standard
CS features weight reduction (bonnet, bucket seats, titanium exhaust)
LCI facelift (2020) brought updated lights and iDrive system
mainEngines: S63B44T4 - 4.4L Petrol (F90 M5 Competition, F90 M5 CS, F90 M5 LCI)
knownFor: CS: 635hp, 0-60mph in 3.0 seconds - fastest-ever M5
Competition: 625hp, the most popular modern M5 variant
CS limited to 1,000 units globally - highly collectable
Same S63B44T4 oil pump and cooling vulnerabilities as standard F90
CS commands significant premium on used market
years.cta: Compare 2018-2023 BMW M5 F90 Competition & CS replacement engine prices

years.preview: Present - G90 M5: Plug-In Hybrid Era
years.description: The seventh-generation M5 embraces electrification with a 727hp plug-in hybrid V8 - the most powerful M5 ever, but also the heaviest at 2.4 tonnes.
keyChanges: First M5 with any form of electrification - PHEV powertrain
S68 V8 (585hp) plus electric motor (197hp) - 727hp combined, 1,000Nm torque
18.6kWh battery provides approx 40 miles electric-only range
8-speed M Steptronic with integrated electric motor
M5 Touring (G99) returns after 14-year absence
mainEngines: S68B44 PHEV - 4.4L Petrol Hybrid (G90 M5, G90 M5 Touring, G99 M5 Touring)
knownFor: 727hp, 1,000Nm torque - most powerful M5 in history
0-60mph in 3.5 seconds (slower than F90 CS due to 2,435kg weight)
PHEV system adds significant complexity to engine replacement
Battery health inspection mandatory for any engine work
Future-proofing - hybrid replacement market will emerge as cars age

faq.h2: BMW M5 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How reliable is the BMW M5 S85 V10 engine and when should I replace the rod bearings?
items.answer: The S85 V10 is notorious for rod bearing failure, with preventative replacement strongly recommended between 60,000-80,000 miles. A well-maintained example with bearings done can easily surpass 150,000 miles.
keyPoints: Rod bearing failure typically occurs between 60,000-85,000 miles if not addressed
Updated BMW bearings (2011+) use harder materials but may damage crankshafts when they fail
Aftermarket bearings (WPC-treated or BE Bearings) offer better clearance and friction reduction
A 153,000-mile E60 M5 with replaced rod bearings still made 507hp on the dyno
Frequent oil analysis is recommended, though newer bearings don't show wear through copper/lead traces
items.cta: Get S85 rod bearing replacement quotes for your E60 M5

items.question: What causes the BMW M5 S63 timing chain to fail and how much does replacement cost?
items.answer: The S63 V8's plastic timing chain guides become brittle with heat and mileage, typically failing between 70,000-100,000 miles. Specialist replacement costs £2,200-£3,200 versus dealer prices of £3,500-£5,000.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattle on cold start is the earliest warning sign
If the chain jumps or snaps, valves contact pistons - engine is terminal
The S63B44 (F10) and S63B44T2 (F10 Competition) are equally vulnerable
Preventive guide replacement adds 60,000-80,000 miles of reliable service
Full rebuilt S63 engine costs £7,500-£13,000 supply & fit from specialists
items.cta: Compare S63 timing chain repair vs replacement for your F10 M5

items.question: Is the BMW M5 E39 S62 V8 reliable and what are its common problems?
items.answer: The E39 M5's 4.9-litre S62 is considered one of BMW's most reliable M engines, but VANOS seal failure is almost guaranteed and causes a distinctive "growling" noise at idle that drives replacement decisions.
keyPoints: VANOS solenoid seals harden and leak oil pressure - very common from 60,000 miles
Symptoms include rough idle, growling noise, hesitation on acceleration
Camshaft position sensors and MAF sensors also fail, triggering check engine lights
M-specific parts are expensive - water pumps cost £250, thermostats £70
A well-maintained S62 can exceed 150,000 miles with proper VANOS maintenance
items.cta: Get S62 VANOS repair or rebuilt engine quotes for your E39 M5

items.question: Where is the engine number located on a BMW M5 and how do I find my engine code?
items.answer: The engine number is typically stamped on the engine block near the thermostat housing or oil filter housing. For accurate identification, use your registration number above - we'll query DVLA and return your exact engine code instantly.
keyPoints: E39 M5 engine number location requires removing the thermostat housing to access
On modern M5s (F10/F90), the engine code is on a label visible from above near the oil filter housing
Engine code format examples: S85B50 (E60), S63B44T4 (F90), S62B50 (E39)
Using your registration is far simpler than finding the physical stamp
DVLA database includes exact engine code for every UK-registered M5
items.cta: Enter your registration above to identify your BMW M5 engine code instantly

items.question: What is the average lifespan of a BMW M5 engine and can it reach 200,000 miles?
items.answer: The average BMW M5 lasts approximately 88,000 miles, with a 0% probability of reaching 200,000 miles according to reliability data. However, this data averages all M5 generations - an E39 with VANOS maintenance will outlast an E60 with neglected bearings.
keyPoints: Average BMW M5 lifespan: 87,764 miles across all generations
Average miles driven per year: 7,341 for M5 owners
200,000-mile M5s are extremely rare - requires meticulous maintenance and proactive bearing replacement
E39 S62 and E34 S38 engines are the most likely to exceed 150,000 miles with proper care
E60 S85 V10s that survive past 100,000 miles typically had rod bearings replaced twice
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW M5 engine prices for high-mileage replacements

items.question: What oil does a BMW M5 use and how often should I change it?
items.answer: M5 engines from 2001-2013 require 10W-60 synthetic oil, specifically formulated for high-performance M engines. Change intervals should not exceed 8,000 miles or annually - many specialists recommend 5,000-6,000 miles for the S85 V10.
keyPoints: Genuine BMW M TwinPower Turbo 10W60 is the factory-specified oil for 2001-2013 M5s
Some specialists prefer Red Line 15W50 for better sheer resistance and bearing protection
S85 V10 engines are particularly sensitive to oil quality and level - check monthly
The latest Shell-produced BMW 10W60 prioritises emissions over wear protection
Short oil change intervals (5,000-6,000 miles) are cheap insurance for rod bearing health
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M5 oil change and engine health check

items.question: How much does a BMW M5 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: BMW M5 engine replacement costs vary dramatically by generation - from £6,000-£11,000 for an E39 S62 to £10,000-£22,000 for an F90 M5 CS. Specialist prices are typically 30-40% lower than main dealer rates.
keyPoints: E39 M5 (S62): rebuilt supply only £6,000-£11,000
E60 M5 (S85 V10): rebuilt supply only £7,000-£14,000
F10 M5 (S63): rebuilt supply only £6,000-£13,000
F90 M5 (S63B44T4): rebuilt supply only £7,000-£22,000 depending on variant
Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on generation complexity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW M5 engine prices for your specific generation

items.question: Which BMW M5 generation has the most reliable engine and which should I avoid?
items.answer: The E39 M5's S62 V8 is widely considered the most reliable M5 engine when VANOS seals are maintained. The E60 M5's S85 V10 is the least reliable - rod bearing failure is almost guaranteed without preventative replacement before 80,000 miles.
keyPoints: E39 M5 (S62): Most reliable, but VANOS seals need replacement as maintenance
E34 M5 (S38): Very reliable straight-six, but timing chain tensioner wears
F90 M5 (S63B44T4): Modern reliability is good, but oil pump drive failures occur
E60 M5 (S85): Most problematic - factor in £1,800-£2,500 for bearings every 60k-80k miles
E28 M5 (M88/3): Classic reliability is good, but parts are increasingly rare and expensive
items.cta: Compare prices across all BMW M5 generations

items.question: Can I swap a different engine into my BMW M5 if the original fails?
items.answer: Engine swaps are technically possible but rarely cost-effective. The M5's electronics, gearbox, and subframes are tightly integrated with its specific engine code. A rebuilt original engine is almost always the smarter financial decision.
keyPoints: ECU, DME, and immobiliser systems are coded to the original engine code
Gearbox compatibility (SMG, DCT, or M Steptronic) is engine-specific
Subframe and mounts differ across generations - not a bolt-in process
Specialist fabrication and custom ECU tuning adds thousands to the bill
Resale value drops significantly with a non-original engine
items.cta: Get quotes for a direct replacement BMW M5 engine - the smart choice

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older BMW M5 like an E39 or E60?
items.answer: Yes, if the chassis and body are in excellent condition. E39 M5s are appreciating assets, and E60 V10s are future classics. Spending £6,000-£14,000 on a rebuilt engine makes financial sense when the car's value exceeds £15,000-£20,000.
keyPoints: E39 M5 values are rising - good examples now sell for £20,000-£35,000
E60 M5s have bottomed out and are entering classic status - values are climbing
A quality rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings adds £3,000-£5,000 to resale value
Cheaper to replace the engine than to buy another M5 of the same generation
Use a specialist rebuild with upgraded components - not a used engine with unknown history
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW M5 engine prices and protect your investment
